001-jp-rfd-process.md.summary: Establishes lightweight Request for Discussion process for proposing and tracking design decisions with clear lifecycle states.

002-using-llms.md.summary: LLMs are tools; you own your output and bear responsibility for its quality regardless of how it was produced.

004-streaming-md-parser-renderer.md.summary: JP streams markdown by segmenting blocks with custom Buffer, then renders them with ANSI-aware terminal renderer.

005-first-class-inquiry-events.md.summary: Record inquiry events in conversation stream with centralized filtering to keep them hidden from LLM providers.

006-turn-scoped-mutations.md.summary: Restrict stream mutations to turn-scoped accessors, preventing invalid event sequences through compile-time guarantees.

007-llamacpp-reasoning.md.summary: Llamacpp provider will drop the openai crate to properly extract reasoning content from all three llama.cpp reasoning formats.

008-knowledge-base.md.summary: Proposes a file-based knowledge base system where assistants retrieve project-specific topics and subjects on-demand via a `learn` tool.

009-stateful-tool-protocol.md.summary: Unifies one-shot and long-running tool execution under a stateful protocol enabling multi-step interactive workflows.

010-pty-infrastructure-and-interactive-t.summary: Proposes jp_pty crate and interactive SDK for building stateful tools that wrap interactive CLI programs.

011-system-message-queue.md.summary: System message queue delivers asynchronous subsystem notifications to the assistant by embedding them in existing conversation messages.

012-incremental-tool-call-argument-strea.summary: Parse tool call arguments incrementally as JSON streams in, emitting progress events before the full object completes.

013-named-query-templates.md.summary: Add reusable, config-defined query templates with interactive prompts for collecting variables before rendering.

014-attachment-handler-guide.md.summary: JP attachment handlers fetch context via URL schemes, supporting both opaque and hierarchical formats with add/remove/list/get trait methods.

015-simplified-attachment-handler-trait..summary: Simplify attachment handlers from stateful (five methods) to stateless (three methods), moving URL tracking to the host.

016-wasm-plugin-architecture.md.summary: Wasm-based plugin architecture enabling third-party extensibility with sandboxed capabilities through WIT interfaces.

017-wasm-attachment-handlers.md.summary: Wasm plugins export an attachment interface, wrapped in a native Handler adapter for transparent integration.

018-typed-inquiry-system.md.summary: Codify prompt types as `Inquiry` enum variants for unified routing, config, and extensibility.

019-non-interactive-mode.md.summary: Configurable fallback behavior for unattended prompts, with safe defaults and output channel separation.

020-parallel-conversations.md.summary: Replace global active conversation with per-session tracking and add conversation locks for parallel terminal work.

021-printer-live-redirection.md.summary: Adds runtime output redirection to Printer via SwapWriters command, enabling mid-stream destination changes without replacing instances.

022-detached-conversations.md.summary: Proposes detached query execution, process registry, and conversation attachment—abandoned and split into RFD 023 and 024.

023-resumable-conversation-turns.md.summary: Split incomplete turns from stream, persist tool results incrementally, resume with --continue flag.

024-detached-query-execution.md.summary: Detached query execution with process registry and queue policy for background conversations; superseded by RFD 027.

025-live-re-attachment-to-detached-queri.summary: Abandoned design for live re-attachment to detached query processes via Unix domain sockets; superseded by RFD 027.

026-agent-loop-extraction.md.summary: Extract the agent turn loop from jp_cli into a new jp_agent crate with trait-based I/O hooks.

027-client-server-query-architecture.md.summary: Restructure `jp query` as unified client-server architecture for foreground, detached, and reattached queries.

028-structured-inquiry-system-for-tool-q.summary: Replace tool re-invocation with async inquiries to avoid token waste and latency when tools need LLM answers.

003-jp-assisted-rfds.md.summary: Propose an `rfd` skill configuration enabling JP to assist RFD writing through research, structure, and collaborative editing—not authorship.

029-scriptable-structured-output.md.summary: Make JP usable in scripts by inferring clean JSON output and adding a concise schema syntax.

030-schema-dsl.md.summary: JP's concise DSL syntax for defining JSON Schema objects via command-line flags with types, descriptions, and nested structures.

031-durable-conversation-storage-with-wo.summary: Make user-local storage the durable source; workspace copies become optional projections for git visibility.

032-grizzly-semantic-search.md.summary: Add FTS5 full-text search, typo tolerance, and local semantic vector search to grizzly's note_search tool.

033-cache-preserving-inquiry-via-tool-us.summary: Replace structured output with a tool-use-based inquiry system to preserve prompt cache and reduce LLM costs.

034-inquiry-specific-assistant-configura.summary: Route inquiries to cheaper models with stable schemas for 5-25x cost reduction via configurable assistant overrides.

035-multi-root-config-load-path-resoluti.summary: Extend `--cfg` path resolution to search user-global, workspace, and user-workspace config roots, merging matches.

042-tool-options.md.summary: Introduces per-tool options field for static user-defined configuration passed to tools at runtime.
